result: You've hit your session limit · resets 3:40am (America/Vancouver)

questions.question: How should I execute the Construction phase (writing the app code + tests)?

options.label: Multi-agent workflow fan-out
options.description: Use the Workflow tool to parallelize code generation across units/engines (U1 interfaces land first as the contract, then fan out). Fastest wall-clock and matches your 'workflow' request, but spawns many subagents and consumes notably more tokens.

options.label: Sequential, inline, autonomous
options.description: I build unit-by-unit myself (U1→U6) in dependency order, running tsc/tests as I go, pausing only if I hit a real problem or product decision. Slower but lower token cost and you stay closer to each step.

options.label: Sequential, pause per unit
options.description: I build unit-by-unit but stop for your review at each unit boundary (after U1 Domain, after U2 Persistence, etc.). Most control, most checkpoints.

questions.question: How much should I aim to complete in this run?

options.label: Full depth-first core
options.description: Push for the complete §9 bar: U1 (Tier-1 engines + all math, tested) + U2 + U3 + U4 + U5 core screens + U6 stub, type-clean with passing tests. Largest single effort.

options.label: Start with U1 Domain Core
options.description: Build + fully test U1 (the pure-domain heart: engines, plate math, e1RM, PR, warm-up, rotation) first, then check in before persistence/UI. De-risks the headline logic early.

options.label: Foundations first (U1+U2)
options.description: Domain Core + Persistence (schema/migrations/repositories) with tests, then check in before services/state/UI.
